rows.notes: Top-left page title 'Sheet.' is clearly written and contains no amounts.
Year '1900.' is clearly written at top; no monetary values present.
Section header 'Assets.' centered and underlined; no amounts in this row.

Handwritten pounds figure appears as '5715' or '5716' — the digit is slightly ambiguous. I read it as 5715 to make the visible group subtotal (5952 0s 4d) consistent. Shillings (4) and pence (6) are clearly aligned in the pound/shilling/pence columns.
Clear reading of 'Library Account' with amounts 236£ 15s 10d; digits are legible and well aligned with column rules.

Subtotal shown on the page as '5952 . 4' (i.e. £5952 0s 4d). The printed subtotal is legible. Because the first entry's hundreds digit was ambiguous (5715 vs 5716), I set that entry to 5715 to match this visible subtotal.
Single clear amount '3000' pounds for Furniture; no shillings/pence shown; alignment with pounds column is unambiguous.
Clear reading '6511 14 2' aligned to £/s/d columns. All currency components are within valid ranges.
Subtotal displayed on page as '9511 14 2', which matches the sum of Furniture (3000) and Investments (6511 14 2). Clear alignment with ruling lines.
Section label 'Due to the College:-' is underlined and introduces the following grouped entries; no amounts on this label row.
Clear amount '134' with no shillings/pence indicated (interpreted as £134 0s 0d). Well aligned in the pounds column.
Clear reading '224 15 3' for Tithes; digits are legible and validate within currency ranges.
Clear reading '53 13 4'; descriptive text 'Houses on long Leases' is legible and aligns with the numeric columns.
Amount printed as '1 /15 .' — interpreted as £1 15s 0d (shillings clearly '15', pence blank/zero). This interpretation is supported by the group subtotal arithmetic on the page.
Clear amount '70' (interpreted as £70 0s 0d); alignment with pounds column is unambiguous.
Displayed subtotal '484 3 7' matches the sum: 134 0 0 + 224 15 3 + 53 13 4 + 1 15 0 + 70 0 0. Column alignment is clear and currency conversions validate.
Amount '18 10 11' is legible. The descriptive word before 'Exhibition Fund' is somewhat unclear in handwriting (reads as 'How' or similar); the monetary figures and alignment are readable.
Name 'Mitchell' is legible. Amount reads as '16 14 .' — interpreted as £16 14s 0d; pence blank implies zero. Minor trailing dot but numeric columns are clear.
Handwritten name appears as 'Loscombe Richards Fund' (scripted; could be 'Loxcombe' variant). Amount '19 12 7' is clearly written and aligned to columns.
Subtotal displayed as '54 17 6' which matches the arithmetic of the three fund lines (18 10 11 + 16 14 0 + 19 12 7). Column alignment is clear.

rows.description: I certify that the Accounts of the College are duly kept in proper books of account and that the Abstracts and Balance Sheet contain a full & true account of the financial condition of the College.
rows.notes: Certification paragraph clearly written in the body of the page; treated as a header/statement (no monetary amounts). Text transcribed verbatim to the extent legible.
Date 'March 8th 1901' is clearly written below the certification text; no monetary values.
Signature block appears as 'W.C. Taddard F.C.O.' — handwriting is a signature but legible enough to transcribe. No amounts present.

Final balance at bottom-right reads as '16002 15 7' (£16,002 15s 7d); digits and alignment are mostly clear though the first digits are large script — interpreted as 16002 to match visible characters.
